def onValueChange(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called whenever the text component's value parameter changes for any reason including typing
	in the text window or changing the parameter directly. The callback is equivalent to the
	onValueChange in the parameter exectute DAT.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonValueChange comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	if parent.ContextPath.extensionsReady: 
		parent.ContextPath.OnTextEntry(value)
	
def onFocus(comp):
	"""
	Called when one of the text components viewers gets keyboard focus. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocus comp:', comp.path)
	return

def onFocusEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when one of the text component's viewers loses keyboard focus. In regular editing mode
	this is right after the contents are saved back to the value parameter. This function is called
	everytime the viewer loses focus, even if the text does not change.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		info: a dictionary containing information about the event including:
			reason: a string indicating what caused the viewer to lose focus e.g. 'enter', 'escape', 'tab', 'unknown'
	
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocusEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	parent.ContextPath.DisplayTextEntry(False)
	
def onTextEdit(comp):
	"""
	Called each time the contents of the text component viewer change i.e. when typing a character,
	deleting text, cutting or pasting. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEdit comp:', comp.path, '- text: ', comp.editText)
	return
	
def onTextEditEnd(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called when the user finishes editing and the value has been changed. Not called in 'Continuous Edit' mode. Use
	onTextEdit and onFocusEnd when using continuous editing.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEditEnd comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return

# me - this DAT
# 
# comp - the List Component that holds this panel
# row - the row number of the cell being updated
# col - the column number of the cell being updated
#
# attribs contains the following members:
#
# text				   str            cell contents
# help                 str       	  help text
#
# textColor            r g b a        font color
# textOffsetX		   n			  horizontal text offset
# textOffsetY		   n			  vertical text offset
# textJustify		   m			  m is one of:  JustifyType.TOPLEFT, JustifyType.TOPCENTER,
#													JustifyType.TOPRIGHT, JustifyType.CENTERLEFT,
#													JustifyType.CENTER, JustifyType.CENTERRIGHT,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMLEFT, JustifyType.BOTTOMCENTER,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMRIGHT
#
# bgColor              r g b a        background color
#
# leftBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside left border color
# rightBorderInColor   r g b a		  inside right border color
# bottomBorderInColor  r g b a		  inside bottom border color
# topBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside top border color
#
# leftBorderOutColor   r g b a		  outside left border color
# rightBorderOutColor  r g b a		  outside right border color
# bottomBorderOutColor r g b a		  outside bottom border color
# topBorderOutColor	   r g b a		  outside top border color
#
# colWidth             w              sets column width
# colStetch            True/False     sets column stretchiness (width is min width)
# rowHeight            h              sets row height
# rowStetch            True/False     sets row stretchiness (height is min height)
# rowIndent            w              offsets entire row by this amount
#
# editable			   int			  number of clicks to activate editing the cell.
# draggable             True/False     allows cell to be drag/dropped elsewhere
# fontBold             True/False     render font bolded
# fontItalic           True/False     render font italicized
# fontSizeX            float		  font X size in pixels
# fontSizeY            float		  font Y size in pixels, if not specified, uses X size
# fontFace             str			  font face, example 'Verdana'
# wordWrap             True/False     word wrap
#
# top                  TOP			  background TOP operator
#
# select   true when the cell/row/col is currently being selected by the mouse
# rollover true when the mouse is currently over the cell/row/col
# radio    true when the cell/row/col was last selected
# focus    true when the cell/row/col is being edited
#
# currently not implemented:
#
# type                str             cell type: 'field' or 'label'
# fieldtype           str             field type: 'float' 'string' or  'integer'
# setpos              True/False x y  set cell absolute when first argument is True
# padding             l r b t         cell padding from each edge, expressed in pixels
# margin              l r b t         cell margin from neighbouring cells, expressed in pixels
#
# fontpath            path            File location to font. Don't use with 'font'
# fontformat          str             font format: 'polygon', 'outline' or 'bitmap'
# fontantialiased     True/False      render font antialiased
# fontcharset         str             font character set
#
# textjustify         h v             left/right/center top/center/bottom
# textoffset          x y             text position offset
#

# called when Reset parameter is pulsed, or on load
# endregion
import TDFunctions as TDF
contextPath = parent.ContextPath
try:
	contextPathExt = ext.ContextPathExt
except:
	pass

def onInitCell(comp, row, col, attribs):
	attribs.sizeInPoints = True

def onInitRow(comp, row, attribs):
	attribs.sizeInPoints = True

def onInitCol(comp, col, attribs):
	attribs.sizeInPoints = True
	# numListCols = ext.ContextPathExt.numListCols
	attribs.colStretch = 0
	if col == 0:
		attribs.colWidth = contextPath.par.Leftmargin
		attribs.text = ''
	elif col == comp.par.cols.eval() - 1:
		attribs.text = ''
		attribs.colStretch = 1
	else:
		info = contextPathExt.displayElementInfo[col - 1]
		attribs.text = info[0]
		attribs.colWidth = info[1]
		if attribs.text == '>>':
			attribs.help = op(contextPath.CurrentOp).currentChild.name

def onInitTable(comp, attribs):
	attribs.sizeInPoints = True
	attribs.rowStretch = 1
	attribs.rowHeight = 0
	if contextPath.par.Font.eval() in contextPath.par.Font.menuNames:
		attribs.fontFace = contextPath.par.Font.menuLabels[
					contextPath.par.Font.menuNames.index(
						contextPath.par.Font.eval())]
	attribs.fontBold = contextPath.par.Bold
	attribs.fontItalic = contextPath.par.Italic
	attribs.fontSizeX = contextPath.par.Fontsize
	attribs.textColor = [p.eval() for p in contextPath.par.Fontcolorr.tuplet]
	attribs.bgColor = [p.eval() for p in contextPath.par.Bgcolorr.tuplet]	

# called during specific events
#
# coords - a named tuple containing the following members:
#   x
#   y
#   u
#   v


def onRollover(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords):
	try:
		attribs = comp.cellAttribs[row, col]
		if col != 0:
			attribs.bgColor = [p.eval() for p in
									contextPath.par.Highlightcolorr.tuplet]
			attribs.textColor = [p.eval() for p in 
									contextPath.par.Highlightfontcolorr.tuplet]
	except: 
		pass
	if prevRow != row or prevCol != col:
		try:
			prevAttribs = comp.cellAttribs[prevRow, prevCol]
			prevAttribs.bgColor = None
			prevAttribs.textColor = None
		except:
			pass

def onSelect(comp, startRow, startCol, startCoords, endRow, endCol, endCoords, 
				start, end):
	if endRow != startRow or endCol != startCol:
		return
	attribs = comp.cellAttribs[endRow, endCol]
	if not end:
		attribs.bgColor = None
		attribs.textColor = None
		ext.ContextPathExt.onPress(endCol, endCoords, comp.panel.rselect)
	elif attribs.rollover and endCol != 0:
		attribs.bgColor = \
				[p.eval() for p in contextPath.par.Highlightcolorr.tuplet]
		attribs.textColor = \
				[p.eval() for p in contextPath.par.Highlightfontcolorr.tuplet]

def onRadio(comp, row, col, prevRow, prevCol):
	return

def onFocus(comp, row, col, prevRow, prevCol):
	return

def onEdit(comp, row, col, val):
	return

# return True if interested in this drop event, False otherwise
def onHoverGetAccept(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	accept = contextPath.par.Setcurrentwithdrop \
			and len(dragItems) == 1 \
			and isinstance(dragItems[0], OP) \
			and TDF.parentLevel(contextPath.RootOp, dragItems[0]) \
															is not None \
			and (contextPathExt.isValidPath(dragItems[0].path) \
					or isinstance(dragItems[0], COMP))
	return accept

def onDropGetAccept(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	return contextPathExt.onDrop(dragItems)

# operatorPath - keep old name for backwards compatibility

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class ContextPathExt:
	"""
	ContextPath is an easy drop-down mouse interface for op selection.
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		#The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp

		#ops
		self.configComp = op('config')
		self.listCOMP = op('listPath')
		self.popMenu = ownerComp.par.Popmenu.eval()
		self.currentArrow = ownerComp.op('itemCurrentArrow')
		self.pathPanel = ownerComp.op('pathPanel')
		self.pathEntry = ownerComp.op('pathEntry')
		self.elementsDAT = ownerComp.op('pathElements')
		self.pathCOMP = ownerComp.op('path')

		# looks
		self.TextTOP = self.configComp.op('textLook')
		self.TextLook = self.TextTOP.par
		self.HighlightTextLook = self.configComp.op('highlightTextLook').par
		self.TextEntryMode = False
		par = ownerComp.par.Currentop
		if par.mode == ParMode.BIND and par.bindMaster:
			master = TDF.bindChain(par)[-1]
			masterVal = master.eval()
			if isinstance(masterVal, OP):
				par.val = master.eval().path
			elif op(masterVal):
				par.val = op(masterVal).path
		if not self.RootOp:
			ownerComp.par.Rootop = '/'
		if not self.CurrentOp and not self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath:
			ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.RootOp.path

		# prep
		self.elementInfo = [] # list of information about elements
		self.firstElementIndex = None
		self.displayElementInfo = []
		self.backArrowInfo = []
		self.currentArrowDisplay = False
		self.Refresh()

	@property
	def RootOp(self):
		rootOp = self.ownerComp.par.Rootop.eval()
		if rootOp:
			return rootOp
		else:
			return op('/')

	@property
	def CurrentOp(self):
		return self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval()

	@property
	def CurrentOperator(self):
		return op(self.CurrentOp)

	def onPress(self, colNum, cellCoords, rightMouse=False):
		"""
		An item in the path listCOMP has been pressed with mouse
		"""
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Rightclickmenu and rightMouse:
			return
		if colNum == 0:
			return
		if colNum > len(self.displayElementInfo):
			# blank area
			self.DisplayTextEntry(True)
			return
		text = self.displayElementInfo[colNum-1][0]
		itemNum = len(self.elementInfo) - len(self.displayElementInfo) \
						+ colNum - 1
		if text == ">>":
			if rightMouse:
				return
			# current arrow button
			if (o:=op(self.ownerComp.par.Currentop).currentChild):
				self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = o.path
			return
		elif text == "<<":
			# path prefix button
			# calculate options
			if rightMouse:
				return
			details = [("/",0)]
			for i, info in enumerate(self.elementInfo[: 1 + \
					len(self.elementInfo) - len(self.displayElementInfo)]):
				if info[0] != '/':
					details = [(info[0], i)] + details
			detailPath = '/' + '/'.join([d[0] for d in reversed(details[:-1])])
			details += [(c, -1) for c in 
									self.getValidChildren(op(detailPath))]
			options = [i[0] for i in details]
			self.popMenu.TempAdjustX = -cellCoords[0] 
			self.popMenu.TempAdjustY = -cellCoords[1] 
			self.popMenu.Open(options, self.backPath, details, 
												dividersAfterItems=['/'])
			return
		elif text == "/":
			# slash dropdown
			if rightMouse:
				return
			pathTo = self.getPathToItem(itemNum)
			options = self.getValidChildren(pathTo)
			if itemNum == 0:
				# root slash
				options = ["/"] + options
			if options:
				ui.refresh()
				options.sort(key=lambda x: x.lower())
				#self.popMenu.SetPlacement(buttonComp=panelValue.owner)
				self.popMenu.TempAdjustX = -cellCoords[0] 
				self.popMenu.TempAdjustY = -cellCoords[1] 
				self.popMenu.Open(options, self.pathSelect, pathTo,
								dividersAfterItems=['/'])
		elif rightMouse:
			# RMB on name - get node menu
			op(self.getPathToItem(itemNum)).openMenu()
		else:
			# LMB on name - drop back to selected op
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = op(self.getPathToItem(itemNum)).path

	def backPath(self, info):
		"""
		BackArrow path menu choice.
		"""
		itemInfo = info['details'][info['index']]
		itemNum = itemInfo[1]
		itemName = itemInfo[0]
		if itemNum == -1:
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.CurrentOp + '/' + itemName
		else:	
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = op(self.getPathToItem(itemNum)).path


	def isValidPath(self, path):
		"""
		Path points to an op that is acceptable after filters applied. If path 
		is an OP, it use the OP's path. If path is None, return False
		"""
		if path is None:
			return
		elif isinstance(path, OP):
			o = path
		else:
			o = op(path)
		l = self.ownerComp.par.Filterkey.eval()
		if callable(l):
			return isinstance(o, self.getFilterClasses()) and l(o)
		else:
			return isinstance(o, self.getFilterClasses())

	def getValidChildren(self, path):
		"""
		Return list of potential children that obey filters, from path
		"""
		if op(path) is None:
			return []
		children = op(path).children
		options = []
		for child in children:
			if not child.expose and not self.ownerComp.par.Includehiddenops:
				continue
			if self.isValidPath(child.path):
				options.append(child.name)
				continue
			if isinstance(child, COMP) and \
					child.findChildren(key=lambda x: self.isValidPath(x.path)):
				options.append(child.name)
		return options

	def getFilterClasses(self):
		classParVal = self.ownerComp.par.Filterclasses.eval().strip()
		if classParVal:
			# allow a list of classes
			filterClasses = tuple(eval(className)
								for className in classParVal.split())
		else:
			# blank means all ops...
			filterClasses = OP
		return filterClasses

	def pathSelect(self, info):
		"""
		A path slash drop down item has been selected.
		"""
		if info['item'] == '/':
			# root slash
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.RootOp.path
		else:
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = \
							op(info['details'] + '/' + info['item']).path

	def getPathToItem(self, itemNum=None):
		"""
		Return a path from the listed items to itemNum, non-inclusive. Defaults
		to full path. Trailing slash not included
		"""
		if itemNum is None:
			itemNum = len(self.elementInfo)
		if itemNum == 0:
			return self.RootOp.path
		path = self.RootOp.path + ''.join([c[0]
							for c in self.elementInfo[:itemNum + 1]])
		if path[-1:] == '/':
			path = path[:-1]
		return path

	def Refresh(self):
		"""
		Refresh the op path view
		"""
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Rootop.eval():
			self.ownerComp.par.Rootop = '/'
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval():
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.RootOp.path
		# force no empty path if we have a root op
		if self.RootOp.path == '/':
			self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath.enable = True
		else:
			self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath.enable = False
		# get current op to display
		current = op(self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval())
		if current is None:
			if self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.val == '' and \
									self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath.eval():
				self.elementInfo.clear()
				self.pathEntry.op('text').par.value = ''
				# turn off text entry
				self.DisplayTextEntry(False)
				return
			else:
				self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.val = '/'
				self.Refresh()
				return
		path = current.path
		rootPath = self.RootOp.path
		if not path.startswith(rootPath):
			path = rootPath
		elif rootPath != '/':
			path = path[len(rootPath):]
		# create element list
		opList = path.split('/')
		elements = ['/']
		currentOp = self.RootOp
		self.currentArrowDisplay = False
		for i, pathOp in enumerate(opList):
			if i == 0:
				if opList == ['','']:
					# path == root
					break
				# skip first entry... always blank for root
				continue
			prevPath = currentOp.path
			currentOp = currentOp.op(pathOp)
			if currentOp is None:
				# op doesn't exist for some reason
				self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.val = prevPath
				self.Refresh()
				return
			elif self.getValidChildren(currentOp.path):
				# valid with options below
				elements.append(pathOp)
				elements.append('/')
			elif self.isValidPath(currentOp.path):
				# valid with no options below
				elements.append(pathOp)
			else:
				# fails Find Class test, end at previous point
				self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.val = prevPath
				self.Refresh()
				return
		if self.ownerComp.par.Currentarrow.eval() and elements[-1] == '/' and \
				currentOp.currentChild and \
				(self.isValidPath(currentOp.currentChild.path) or
			 	 self.getValidChildren(currentOp.currentChild.path)):
			self.currentArrowDisplay = True
		# turn off text entry
		self.DisplayTextEntry(False)
		# set up elementInfo
		pathSpacing = self.ownerComp.par.Pathspacing.eval()
		minWidth = 33
		elementInfo = [] # [[text, width, minWidth]]
		for element in reversed([e for e in elements] + \
							(['>>'] if self.currentArrowDisplay else [])):
			width = self.TextTOP.evalTextSize(element)[0] + pathSpacing * 2
			minWidth += width
			elementInfo.append((element, width, minWidth))
		self.backArrowInfo = ('<<',
						self.TextTOP.evalTextSize('<<')[0] + pathSpacing * 2, 0)
		self.elementInfo = list(reversed(elementInfo))
		self.checkMinWidths(forceUpdate=True)

	def checkMinWidths(self, forceUpdate=False):
		firstElementIndex = len(self.elementInfo)
		for info in reversed(self.elementInfo):
			if self.ownerComp.width > info[2]:
				firstElementIndex -= 1
		if not forceUpdate and firstElementIndex == self.firstElementIndex:
			return
		self.firstElementIndex = firstElementIndex
		if firstElementIndex > 0:
			self.displayElementInfo = \
					[self.backArrowInfo] + self.elementInfo[firstElementIndex:]
		else:
			self.displayElementInfo = self.elementInfo
		self.listCOMP.par.cols = 2 + len(self.displayElementInfo)
		self.listCOMP.par.reset.pulse()		

	def DisplayTextEntry(self, display):
		"""
		If display is true, show text entry field for path. Otherwise show
		automated drop-down entry
		"""
		if op(self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval()):
			if self.RootOp.path == '/':
				textVal = self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval()

			else:
				textVal = self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval()[
												len(self.RootOp.path):] + '/'
			self.pathEntry.panel.field = self.pathEntry.op('text').par.value = \
				textVal
		elif self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath.eval():
			self.pathEntry.panel.field = self.pathEntry.op('text').par.value = \
									self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = ''
		else:
			self.pathEntry.panel.field = self.pathEntry.op('text').par.value = \
																			'/'
			self.currentOp = self.RootOp.path
		
		if display:
			self.listCOMP.par.hfillweight = 0
			self.pathEntry.par.hfillweight = 1
			# self.pathEntry.cook(force=True, recurse=True)
			self.pathEntry.op('text').setKeyboardFocus(selectAll=True)
		else:
			self.listCOMP.par.hfillweight = 1
			self.pathEntry.par.hfillweight = 0
		self.listCOMP.cook(force=True)
		self.pathEntry.cook(force=True)
		self.TextEntryMode = display

	def onDrop(self, dropItems):
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Setcurrentwithdrop:
			return
		dropPath = dropItems[0].path
		if self.isValidOp(op(dropPath)): 		
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = dropPath
			return True

	def isValidOp(self, o):
		'''
		returns True if o is either the RootOp or a child of it
		'''
		return TDF.parentLevel(self.RootOp, o) or o == self.RootOp

	def OnTextEntry(self, text):
		"""
		Value entered via text entry
		"""
		originalText = text
		if text.strip():
			text = text.strip()
			if text[0] == '/':
				text = text[1:]
			while self.RootOp.op(text) is None and text:
				text = text[:text.rfind('/')]
			if text == '':
				o = None
				if self.ownerComp.par.Allowshortcuts \
							and originalText.startswith(('op.', 'parent.')) \
							and ' ' not in originalText:
					try:
						o = self.CurrentOperator.evalExpression(originalText)
					except:
						if not self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval():
							try:
								o = root.evalExpression(originalText)
							except:
								pass
				if o is not None and self.isValidOp(o):
					self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = o.path
				else:
					self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.RootOp.path
			else:
				self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.RootOp.op(text).path

		elif self.ownerComp.par.Allowemptypath.eval():
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = ''
		else:
			self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = self.RootOp.path

	def OnParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		"""
		A parameter value has changed
		"""
		if par.name == 'Currentop':
			if par.mode == ParMode.BIND and par.bindMaster:
				master = TDF.bindChain(par)[-1]
				masterVal = master.eval()
				if isinstance(masterVal, OP):
					par.val = master.eval().path
				elif op(masterVal):
					par.val = op(masterVal).path

			self.OnTextEntry(par.eval())
			self.Refresh()
		elif par.name == 'Rootop':
			if not par.eval():
				par.val = '/'
			if not self.ownerComp.par.Currentop.eval().startswith(
															self.RootOp.path):
				self.ownerComp.par.Currentop = par.val
			self.Refresh()
		elif par.name == 'Filterclasses':
			try:
				self.getFilterClasses()
			except:
				par.val = prev
				raise ValueError("Find Class must be a valid subclass of OP. "
								 "Got: " + val)
			self.Refresh()
		elif par.name == 'Popmenu':
			self.popMenu = par.eval()
		elif par.isCustom:
			self.Refresh()

"""
All callbacks for this lister go in this DAT. For a list of available callbacks,
see:

https://www.derivative.ca/wiki099/
							index.php?title=Lister_Custom_COMP#Custom_Callbacks
"""

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDJSON = op.TDModules.mod.TDJSON
showInPane = TDF.showInPane
opFams = (COMP, TOP, CHOP, SOP, MAT, DAT)
ownerComp = None
nextCurrent = None

def onInit(info):
	global ownerComp
	ownerComp = info['listerExt'].ownerComp
	info['listerExt'].DefaultPane = "Floating"
#	debug(parent.OPTree.storage['CurrentOP'])
	TDF.createProperty(info['listerExt'], 'CurrentOP',
					   value=op(parent.OPTree.storage['CurrentOP']),
					   dependable=True)
	TDF.createProperty(info['listerExt'], 'CurrentOPRow', value=None,
					   dependable=True)
	TDF.createProperty(info['listerExt'], 'Checked', value=set(),
					   dependable=True)

	ownerComp.par.Filtercols = ''

def onRefresh(info):
	component = parent.OPTree.par.Component.eval()
	ext = info['listerExt']
	for row, d in enumerate(ext.Data):
		rowOp = getRowOP(d)
		if (rowOp and rowOp == ext.CurrentOP) \
				or (ext.CurrentOP is None and rowOp == component):
			setCurrentOPRow(row)
	if ext.initialized:
		refreshChecks()
		# setup button columns
		for colName in ['Parameters','Network','Viewer','Expando']:
			colNum = ownerComp.ColumnDict[colName]
			attribs = ownerComp.colAttribs[colNum]
			attribs.fontFace = 'Material Design Icons'		

def onReloadInput(info):
	# This fixes loopy effects of deleted nodes that are expanded in the tree.
	# There may be an error for one frame before these take effect
	listerExt = info['listerExt']
	for root, paths in listerExt.Expanded.items():
		if not op(root):
			run('args[0].Expanded.myMainDep.modified()', listerExt,
				delayFrames=1)
		else:
			for path in paths:
				if not op(path):
					run('args[0].Expanded.myMainDep.modified()', listerExt,
						delayFrames=1)


def onInitTable(info):
	if hasattr(info['listerExt'], '_CurrentOPRow'):
		row = info['listerExt']._CurrentOPRow.val
		if row is not None:
			try:
				ownerComp.SetRowOverlay(row, currentColor(), True)
			except:
				info['listerExt'].CurrentOPRow = None
	# set up Type help
	columnDict = info['listerExt'].columnDict
	if 'Type' in columnDict:
		typeCol = columnDict['Type']
	else:
		return
	for row in range(1, info['listerExt'].nRows):
		# debug(ownerComp.Data[row])
		try:
			ownerComp.cellAttribs[row, typeCol].help = \
						op(int(ownerComp.Data[row]['rowObject']['id'])).label
		except:
			if not ownerComp.Data[row]['rowObject']:
				# object deleted
				pass
			else:
				raise


def onPostInit(info):
	refreshChecks()

def getRowOP(rowData):
	if rowData and rowData['rowObject'] \
							and not rowData['rowObject'].get('isTag', False):
		return op(int(rowData['rowObject']['id']))

def onEditEnd(info):
	global nextCurrent
	rowOp = getRowOP(info.get('rowData'))
	if rowOp:
		id = ownerComp.CurrentOP.id
		rowOp.name = info['cellText']
		run('op("' + ownerComp.TreeLister.path +
										'").CurrentOP = op(' + str(id) + ')',
				delayFrames=4, delayRef=op.TDResources)
		nextCurrent = op(id)

def onConvertData(info):
	global nextCurrent
	if nextCurrent:
		ownerComp.FromPathsSelectRows([nextCurrent.path])
		ownerComp.CurrentOP = nextCurrent
		nextCurrent = None
	#debug(info['data'][1])

def onRowObjectToWorkingData(info):
	item = info['convertedRow']
	lister = info['listerExt']
	columnDict = lister.columnDict
	if item is None or item[-1] is None:
		return item
	# debug(item)
	try:
		rowObject = op(int(item[-1]['id']))
	except:
		return item
	if 'Tags' in columnDict:
		tagCol = columnDict['Tags']
		item[tagCol] = ' '.join(rowObject.tags)
	if 'Type' in columnDict:
		typeCol = columnDict['Type']
		if parent.OPTree.par.Typecolumn.eval() == 'Abbreviations':
			label = rowObject.icon
		else:
			label = rowObject.label
		# color op cells
		for family in opFams:
			if isinstance(rowObject, family):
				top = ownerComp.par.Configcomp.eval(). \
					op(family.__name__.lower())
				item[typeCol] = [top.par.bgcolorr.eval(),
								 top.par.bgcolorg.eval(),
								 top.par.bgcolorb.eval(),
								 top.par.bgalpha.eval(),
								 label]
				break
	return item

def onClickViewer(info):
	# open viewer
	o = getRowOP(info.get('rowData'))
	if o:
		o.openViewer()

def onClickParameters(info):
	# open parameters
	o = getRowOP(info.get('rowData'))
	if o:
		o.openParameters()

def onClickNetwork(info):
	# open network
	o = getRowOP(info.get('rowData'))
	if o:
		showInPane(o, ownerComp.DefaultPane)

def onClickCheck(info):
	checked = ownerComp.Checked
	clickID = int(info['rowData']['rowObject']['id'])
	newState = not (clickID in checked)
	if info['row'] in ownerComp.SelectedRows:
		checkRows = ownerComp.SelectedRows
	else:
		checkRows = [info['row']]
	for row in checkRows:
		id = int(ownerComp.Data[row]['rowObject']['id'])
		if newState:
			checked.add(id)
		else:
			checked.discard(id)
		# check or uncheck all children
		for childID in treeChildren(id):
			if newState:
				checked.add(childID)
			else:
				checked.discard(childID)
	refreshChecks()

def treeChildren(id):
	"""
	return ids of children of op(id) that are in the tree (respecting filters)
	:return: list
	"""
	children = []
	opTable = parent.OPTree.op('allChildren')
	pathCol = opTable.col('path')[0].col
	idCol = opTable.col('id')[0].col
	checkPath = op(id).path
	if parent.OPTree.par.Includewired:
		for row in opTable.rows()[1:]:
			path = row[pathCol].val
			if path == '/':
				break
			if checkPath == '/':
				children.append(int(row[idCol].val))
				continue
			elif path.startswith(checkPath + '/'):
				children.append(int(row[idCol].val))
				continue
			checkOP = op(path)
			while checkOP != root:
				try:
					checkOP = checkOP.inputCOMPs[0] \
									if checkOP.inputCOMPs else checkOP.parent()
				except:
					checkOP = checkOP.parent()
				checkOPPath = checkOP.path
				if checkOPPath == checkPath:
					children.append(int(row[idCol].val))
					break
				if checkPath.startswith(checkOPPath):
					break
	else:
		rowNum = opTable.row(str(id))[0].row + 1
		while rowNum < opTable.numRows:
			row = opTable.row(rowNum)
			path = row[pathCol].val
			if not path.startswith(checkPath) or path[len(checkPath)] != '/':
				break
			# print(row[pathCol].val)
			children.append(int(row[idCol].val))
			rowNum += 1
	return children

def refreshChecks():
	if not hasattr(ownerComp.ext, 'ListerExt'):
		return
	listerExt = ownerComp.ext.ListerExt
	checkCol = listerExt.ColumnDict.get('Check')
	if not checkCol:
		return
	checked = listerExt.Checked
	data = listerExt.Data
	for i,row in enumerate(data):
		if i == 0:
			continue
		try:
			id = int(row['rowObject']['id'])
		except:
			# timing off
			continue
		if isinstance(op(id), COMP):
			children = treeChildren(id)
			if children:
				checkChildren = \
							[child for child in children if child in checked]
				if len(checkChildren) == len(children):
					row['Check'] = 1
					checked.add(id)
				elif any(checkChildren):
					row['Check'] = 2
					checked.discard(id)
				else:
					row['Check'] = 0
					checked.discard(id)
			else:
				row['Check'] = 1 if id in checked else 0
		else:
			row['Check'] = 1 if id in checked else 0
		listerExt.InitCell(i, checkCol)
		listerExt._Checked.modified()

def onClickHeaderCheck(info):
	checks = all(d['Check'] == 1 for d in ownerComp.Data[1:])
	if checks:
		for d in info['ownerComp'].Data[1:]:
			d['Check'] = 0
			ownerComp.Checked.remove(int(d['rowObject']['id']))
	else:
		for d in info['ownerComp'].Data[1:]:
			d['Check'] = 1
			ownerComp.Checked.add(int(d['rowObject']['id']))
	ownerComp.ext.ListerExt._Checked.modified()
	ownerComp.Refresh()

	colName = ownerComp.ColNumDict[info['col']]
	if colName == 'Network':
		paneChooser = ownerComp.ConfigComp.op('paneSelect')
		o = getRowOP(info.get('rowData'))
		if o:
			paneChooser.ViewOp(o,  callback=paneSelected,
							   callbackArgs=ownerComp)
		paneChooser.setFocus()
	else:
		return True # allow clicks

def onMouseDown(info):
	ownerComp.ext.TreeListerExt.lastMouseDownInfo = info

def paneSelected(info, ownerComp):
	ownerComp.DefaultPane = info

	if not parent.OPTree or parent.OPTree.par.Rightclickmenu:
		o = getRowOP(info.get('rowData'))
		if o:
			o.openMenu()

def onSelectRow(info):
	if ownerComp.ext.TreeListerExt.CurrentOP is None or \
				ownerComp.ext.TreeListerExt.CurrentOP.id not in \
					[int(ro['id']) for ro in ownerComp.SelectedRowObjects]:
		setCurrentOPRow(info['row'])

def onDeselectRow(info):
	if ownerComp.SelectedRowObjects:
		currentOP = ownerComp.ext.TreeListerExt.CurrentOP
		if currentOP is None or currentOP.id not in \
					[int(ro['id']) for ro in ownerComp.SelectedRowObjects]:
			setCurrentOPRow( ownerComp.SelectedRows[-1])
	else:
		setCurrentOPRow(None)

def onRemovedSelectedRow(info):
	path = info['rowData']['rowObject']['path']
	if ownerComp.FromPathGetRowNum(path):
		ownerComp.FromPathsSelectRows([path])
	else:
		onDeselectRow(info)

def currentColor():
	return [getattr(ownerComp.ConfigComp.op('rowCurrent').par, p).eval() \
				 	for p in ['bgcolorr', 'bgcolorg', 'bgcolorb', 'bgalpha']] \
		   			+ [48]

def setCurrentOPRow(row):
	if not hasattr(ownerComp.ext, 'ListerExt'):
		return
	listerExt = ownerComp.ext.ListerExt
	if listerExt.CurrentOPRow is not None:
		try:
			listerExt.SetRowOverlay(listerExt.CurrentOPRow, currentColor(),
									False)
		except:
			pass
	listerExt.CurrentOPRow = row
	if row is None or row == -1:
		listerExt.CurrentOP = None
	else:
		if len(listerExt.Data) > row:
			if row not in listerExt.SelectedRows:
				listerExt.SelectRow(row)
			listerExt.CurrentOP = getRowOP(listerExt.Data[row])
			# ownerComp.SetRowLook(row, 'rowCurrent', True, 50)
			#debug(ownerComp.ext.TreeListerExt.CurrentOPRow, currentColor())
			try:
				listerExt.SetRowOverlay(listerExt.CurrentOPRow, currentColor(),
										True)
			except:
				pass
		# ownerComp.SetRowOverlay(listerExt.CurrentOPRow,
			# 						currentColor(), True)
		elif listerExt.SelectedRowObjects:
			newSelectRow = listerExt.SelectedRows[-1]
			listerExt.CurrentOP = getRowOP(listerExt.Data[newSelectRow])
			setCurrentOPRow(newSelectRow)
		else:
			setCurrentOPRow(None)
	parent.OPTree.storage['CurrentOP'] = listerExt.CurrentOP.path \
											if listerExt.CurrentOP else None
	# if row:
	# 	print(listerExt.overlays[(row, 0)])

def checkDrop(info):
	par = ui.rolloverPar
	if par:
		lastMouseDownInfo = ownerComp.ext.TreeListerExt.lastMouseDownInfo
		lastOP = getRowOP(lastMouseDownInfo.get('rowData'))
		if par.isOP and lastOP:
			par.expr = TDF.getShortcutPath(par.owner, lastOP)

showInPane = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions.showInPane

@property
def Selecting(self):
	return self.selecting

class PaneSelectExt:
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp		
		self.table = op('paneTable')
		self.window = op('window')
		self.newPaneButton = op('newPaneButton')
		self.title = op('title')
		self.paneButtons= []
		self.callback = None
		self.callbackArgs = None
		self.autoClose = False
		self.selecting = False
		self.opToOpen = None

	def Open(self, callback, callbackArgs=None, opToOpen=None,
			height=60, autoClose=True):
		"""
		Open(callback, height=30, autoClose=True)
			Create all the pane buttons. PaneSelectors are meant to be popups 
			that activate on mouseup.
		callback: when pane is selected, callback( paneOrNoneOr'Floating')
			will be	called
		height: selector's height in pixels. Width is calculated from this
		autoClose: if True, destroy selector when pane is picked
		"""
		self.autoClose = autoClose
		self.callback = callback
		self.callbackArgs = callbackArgs
		self.opToOpen = opToOpen
		self.ownerComp.par.h = self.selectorHeight = height
		self.selecting = True
		#delete old buttons
		for o in parent().children:
			try:
				if o.par.clone in [op('selectablePane'), op('lockedPane')]:
					o.destroy()
			except:
				pass
		self.paneButtons = []
		# figure out touch window dimensions
		bottom = 10000
		top = 0
		right = 0	
		left = 10000
		paneButtonsHeight = self.selectorHeight - self.newPaneButton.par.h - \
									self.title.par.h
		for i, pane in enumerate(ui.panes):
			# ignore floating panes
			if pane.bottomLeft.y == 0: # this is the only way to tell for now
				continue
			# check for maximized
			if any([pane.maximize for pane in ui.panes]):
				if not pane.maximize:
					continue
			# figure out dimensions
			left = min(pane.bottomLeft.x, left)
			bottom = min(pane.bottomLeft.y, bottom)
			right = max(pane.topRight.x, right)
			top = max(pane.topRight.y, top)
			# create buttons
			if pane.type == PaneType.NETWORKEDITOR:
				buttonType = op('selectablePane')
			else:
				buttonType = op('lockedPane')
			paneButton = parent().copy(buttonType)
			paneButton.par.clone = buttonType
			# general paneButton adjustments
			paneButton.par.PaneIndex = i
			paneButton.nodeX = pane.bottomLeft.x
			paneButton.nodeY = pane.topRight.y
			self.paneButtons.append(paneButton)
		# store layout data for Touch window
		width = right - left
		height = top - bottom
		# calculate our target width
		self.ownerComp.par.w = self.selectorWidth = paneButtonsHeight * (
															width / height)
		# size the selector paneButton
		for button in self.paneButtons:
			pane = ui.panes[button.par.PaneIndex]
			# size of button
			# fancy math is to add rounded off portion of position back to size
			buttonX = self.selectorWidth * ((pane.bottomLeft.x - left) / width)
			button.par.x = roundF(buttonX)
			buttonY = paneButtonsHeight * ((pane.bottomLeft.y - bottom)\
																	/ height)
			button.par.y = roundF(buttonY)
			buttonW = (buttonX - button.par.x) + self.selectorWidth * \
							((pane.topRight.x - pane.bottomLeft.x) / width)
			button.par.w = roundF(buttonW)
			buttonH = (buttonY - button.par.y) + paneButtonsHeight * \
						   ((pane.topRight.y - pane.bottomLeft.y) / height)
			button.par.h = roundF(buttonH)
			button.par.display = True
		self.window.par.winopen.pulse()
		self.ownerComp.setFocus()

	def ViewOp(self, operator, callback=None, callbackArgs=None, height=60):
		"""
		Same as 'Open' method, but will automatically create a view of the given
		operator in the selected pane and close PaneSelect.

		Get a callback anyway, if set.
		"""
		self.Open(callback=callback, callbackArgs=callbackArgs,
				  opToOpen=operator)

	def doCallback(self, info):
		if self.callback:
			if self.callbackArgs:
				self.callback(info, self.callbackArgs)
			else:
				self.callback(info)
			self.callback = None

	def OnClick(self, button):
		"""
		Callback from pane buttons.
		"""
		if self.selecting:
			if button == self.newPaneButton:
				selected = 'Floating'
			else:
				selected = ui.panes[button.par.PaneIndex.eval()]
			if self.opToOpen:
				showInPane(self.opToOpen, selected)
			self.doCallback(selected)
			self.selecting = False
		if self.autoClose:
			self.window.par.winclose.pulse()

	def OnLostFocus(self):
		if self.autoClose:
			self.doCallback(None)
			self.window.par.winclose.pulse()

from math import floor
def roundF(f):
	return floor(0.6 + f)

*�����������������S# me - this DAT
# 
# comp - the replicator component which is cooking
# allOps - a list of all replicants, created or existing
# newOps - the subset that were just created
# template - table DAT specifying the replicator attributes
# master - the master operator
#
parentLevel = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions.parentLevel
filterPars = ['callbacks', 'casesensitive', 'combinefilters', 'namefilter',
			  'typefilter', 'tagsfilter', 'textfilter', 'parnamefilter',
			  'parvaluefilter', 'parexpressionfilter', 'parnondefaultonly',
			  'objects', 'panels', 'other', 'tops', 'chops', 'sops',
			  'mats', 'dats']

def onReplicate(comp, allOps, newOps, template, master):

	# Greg said to put the following into an expression on the master operator
	# for c in allOps:
	# 	c.par.component = template[c.digits, 0] if parentLevel(op('opfindComponent').par.component.eval(), op(template[c.digits, 0])) is not None else ''
		
	for c in newOps:
		# c.par.maxdepth.expr = "mod.depthTest.maxdepth(me)"
		if parent.OPTree.par.Filterrootsonly:
			for p in filterPars:
				getattr(c.par, p).mode = ParMode.CONSTANT
	return

2
*�����������������W# me - this DAT.
# 
# dat - the changed DAT
# rows - a list of row indices
# cols - a list of column indices
# cells - the list of cells that have changed content
# prev - the list of previous string contents of the changed cells
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the DAT Execute DAT.
# 
# If rows or columns are deleted, sizeChange will be called instead of row/col/cellChange.

tree = op('opFindTreeLister')
def onTableChange(dat):
	current = dat[0,0].val
	if current != 'None':
		row = tree.FromPathGetRowNum(current)
		if row:
			tree.SelectRow(row, True)
			op('opFindTreeLister').ext.CallbacksExt.callbackDat.module.setCurrentOPRow(row)
	else:
		tree.SelectedRows = []
	return

def onRowChange(dat, rows):
	return

def onColChange(dat, cols):
	return

def onCellChange(dat, cells, prev):
	return

def onSizeChange(dat):
	return

#
# comp is the List Component that holds this panel.
# row is the row number of the cell being updated.
# col is the column number of the cell being updated.
#
# attribs contains the following members:
#
# text				   str			cell contents.
# help				 str			 help text
#
# textColor			r g b a		font color
# textOffsetX		   n			  horizontal text offset
# textOffsetY		   n			  vertical text offset
# textJustify		   m			  m is one of:  JustifyType.TOPLEFT, JustifyType.TOPCENTER,
#													JustifyType.TOPRIGHT, JustifyType.CENTERLEFT,
#													JustifyType.CENTER, JustifyType.CENTERRIGHT,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMLEFT, JustifyType.BOTTOMCENTER,
#													JustifyType.BOTTOMRIGHT
#
# bgColor			  r g b a		background color
#
# leftBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside left border color
# rightBorderInColor   r g b a		  inside right border color
# topBorderInColor	   r g b a		  inside top border color
# bottomBorderInColor  r g b a		  inside bottom border color
#
# leftBorderOutColor   r g b a		  outside left border color
# rightBorderOutColor  r g b a		  outside right border colorh
# topBorderOutColor	   r g b a		  outside top border color
# bottomBorderOutColor r g b a		  outside bottom border color
#
# colWidth			 w			  sets column width
# colStetch			True/False	 sets column stretchiness (width is min width)
# rowHeight			h			  sets row height
#
# editable			   bool			  contents are editable
# fontBold			 True/False	 render font bolded
# fontItalic		   True/False	 render font italicized
# fontSizeX			float		  font X size in pixels
# fontSizeX			float		  font Y size in pixels, if not specified, uses X size
# fontFace			 str			  font face, example 'Verdana'
# wordWrap			 True/False	 word wrap
#
# image				TOP			  background TOP operator
#
# select   true when the cell/row/col is currently being selected by the mouse.
# rollover true when the mouse is currently over the cell/row/col.
# radio	true when the cell/row/col was last selected.
# focus	true when the cell/row/col is being edited.
#
# currently not implemented:
#
# type				str			 cell type: 'field' or 'label'
# fieldtype		   str			 field type: 'float' 'string' or  'integer'
# setpos			  True/False x y  set cell absolute when first argument is True
# padding			 l r b t		 cell padding from each edge, expressed in pixels
# margin			  l r b t		 cell margin from neighbouring cells, expressed in pixels
#
# fontpath			path			File location to font. Don't use with 'font'
# fontformat		  str			 font format: 'polygon', 'outline' or 'bitmap'
# fontantialiased	 True/False	  render font antialiased
# fontcharset		 str			 font character set
#
# textjustify		 h v			 left/right/center top/center/bottom
# textoffset		  x y			 text position offset
#

#called when Reset parameter is pulsed, or on load

def onInitCell(comp, row, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitRow(comp, row, attribs):
	return


def onInitCol(comp, col, attribs):
	return


def onInitTable(comp, attribs):
	try:
		listerExt = ext.ListerExt
	except:
		pass
	else:
		listerExt.onInitTable(attribs)

	return

#called during specific events
#
# coords is a named tuple containing the following members:
#   x
#   y
#   u
#   v


def onRollover(comp, row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol, prevcoords):
	# debug('rollover', row, col)
	ext.ListerExt.onRollover(row, col, coords, prevrow, prevcol,
										prevcoords)
	return


def onSelect(comp, startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow, endcol, endcoords,
			 start, end):
	# print(me, 'onSelect', comp, startrow, start, end)
	ext.ListerExt.onSelect(startrow, startcol, startcoords, endrow,
										endcol, endcoords, start, end)
	return


def onRadio(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	# print(onRadio, comp, row, col)
	return


def onFocus(comp, row, col, prevrow, prevcol):
	ext.ListerExt.onFocus( row, col, prevrow, prevcol)
	return

def onEdit(comp, row, col, val):
	# print("onEdit", row, col, val)
	ext.ListerExt.onEdit( row, col, val)
	return

# return True if interested in this drop event, False otherwise
def onHover(comp, row, col, coords,	prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		retVal = ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(True, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)
		return retVal

def onDrop(comp, row, col, coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems):
	if parent.Lister.par.drop.eval() != 'dropno' \
			and parent.Lister.fetch('legacyDragDrop', 0):
		return ext.ListerExt.onDropOrHover(False, comp, row, col,
								coords, prevRow, prevCol, prevCoords, dragItems)

*�����������������# callbacks for when associated Panel is being dropped on

def onHoverStartGetAccept(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when comp needs to know if dragItems are acceptable as a drop.

	Args:
		comp: the panel component being hovered over
		info: A dictionary containing all info about hover, including:
			dragItems: a list of objects being dragged over comp
			callbackPanel: the panel Component pointing to this callback DAT

	Returns:
		True if comp can receive dragItems
	"""
	#debug('\nonHoverStartGetAccept comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return True # accept what is being dragged

def onHoverEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when dragItems leave comp's hover area.

	Args:
		comp: the panel component being hovered over
		info: A dictionary containing all info about hover, including:
			dragItems: a list of objects being dragged over comp
			callbackPanel: the panel Component pointing to this callback DAT
	"""
	#debug('\nonHoverEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)

def onDropGetResults(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when comp receives a drop of dragItems. This will only be called if
	onHoverStartGetAccept has returned True for these dragItems.

	Args:
		comp: the panel component being dropped on
		info: A dictionary containing all info about drop, including:
			dragItems: a list of objects being dropped on comp
			callbackPanel: the panel Component pointing to this callback DAT

	Returns:
		A dictionary of results with descriptive keys. Some possibilities:
			'droppedOn': the object receiving the drop
			'createdOPs': list of created ops in order of drag items
			'dropChoice': drop menu choice selected
			'modified': object modified by drop
	"""
	debug('\nonDropGetResults comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return {'droppedOn': comp}

# callbacks for when associated Panel is being dragged

def onDragStartGetItems(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when information about dragged items is required.

	Args:
		comp: the panel clicked on to start drag
		info: A dictionary containing all info about drag
			callbackPanel: the panel Component pointing to this callback DAT

	Returns:
		A list of dragItems: [object1, object2, ...]
	"""
	dragItems = [comp.CurrentOP] if comp.extensionsReady else [] # drag the comp itself
	#debug('\nonDragStartGetItems comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return dragItems

def onDragEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when a drag action ends.

	Args:
		comp: the panel clicked on to start drag
		info: A dictionary containing all info about drag, including:
			accepted: True if the drag was accepted, False if not
			dropResults: a dict of drop results. This is the return value of 
				onDropGetResults
			dragItems: the original dragItems for the drag
			callbackPanel: the panel Component pointing to this callback DAT
	"""
	#debug('\nonDragEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
